pattern: A number survives a 180-degree rotation only if every digit is rotatable, and it becomes a different number only if at least one digit actually changes, so counting reduces to a per-digit classification over each value in the range.
hints: Sort the digits into three buckets: ones that stay the same, ones that turn into a different valid digit, and ones that become garbage.
A number counts only if it contains no garbage digit and at least one digit that flips to something different.
For each value check its digits: reject immediately on a 3, 4, or 7, and mark it good if it also contains at least one 2, 5, 6, or 9; tally the goods.

edge_inputs.description: first example, four good numbers up to ten
one has no flipping digit so zero good
two is the first good number
numbers made only of self-rotating digits do not count
just below a value containing an invalid digit

stress.description: random upper bounds across the full allowed range
stress.note: the reference counts directly so any n within range is fast
